text: Can the system maintain a 10s polling cycle across 50k devices (with retry limits defined)?
Is the O(n²) control computation mitigated (batching, sharding, or approximation)?
Is a data aggregation strategy defined with back-pressure and sampling guarantees?
≤ 3 min per device (upload, trigger, restart) under target load?
Resource SLOs (CPU/RAM/disk) defined for each tier?
SNMPv3, secure credential storage, RBAC enforced?
Tenant-scoped tokens and egress policy to prevent cross-tenant access?
Can platform-specific behavior be enabled through configuration rather than source-code modification?
Are environment-specific settings externalized from the application binary?
Can the same deployment artifact be deployed across multiple supported environments?
Does the architecture support tenant/client-specific customization without code duplication?
Are feature flags or plugin mechanisms available for client-specific functionality?
Can client-specific capabilities be isolated from the core application?
Can platform-specific dependencies be isolated without affecting other deployment targets?
Is the build pipeline capable of producing platform-specific artifacts when required?
Are security controls consistently validated across all deployment variants?
